examples.title: Recursive DNS Query Algorithm
examples.description: Demonstrates how a recursive DNS resolver works
examples.code:
FUNCTION RecursiveQuery(domainName, queryType)
 // Check local cache first
 IF cache.contains(domainName, queryType) THEN
 RETURN cache.get(domainName, queryType)
 END IF
 
 // Start with root server
 currentServer = GetRootServer()
 
 WHILE TRUE DO
 // Query current server
 response = currentServer.query(domainName, queryType)
 
 IF response.isAuthoritative THEN
 // Found the answer
 cache.store(domainName, queryType, response)
 RETURN response
 ELSIF response.referral THEN
 // Follow the referral
 currentServer = response.nextServer
 END IF
 END WHILE
END FUNCTION
examples.explanation: This algorithm shows the recursive resolution process: check cache, query root server, follow referrals through TLD to authoritative server, and cache the result.

examples.title: DNS Zone Transfer (AXFR)
examples.description: Shows how secondary servers synchronize zone data
examples.code:
// Primary Server
FUNCTION HandleZoneTransfer(zoneName, clientIP)
 IF NOT authorizedForTransfer(clientIP) THEN
 RETURN REFUSED
 END IF
 
 records = GetZoneRecords(zoneName)
 RETURN records
END FUNCTION

// Secondary Server
FUNCTION SyncWithPrimary(primaryIP)
 serial = GetCurrentSerial()
 
 // Query for SOA to check if update needed
 soa = Query(primaryIP, zoneName, SOA)
 
 IF soa.serial > serial THEN
 // Zone needs update - request full transfer
 fullZone = TransferZone(primaryIP, zoneName)
 UpdateLocalZone(fullZone)
 END IF
END FUNCTION
examples.explanation: Zone transfer uses TCP port 53 (AXFR protocol) to replicate DNS records between primary and secondary servers, ensuring consistency.
